title: Public Identity, Privacy, Security, and Reputation Boundaries · Career Decision Retrieval
summary: A delayed hiring review reconstructs public identity, privacy, security, and reputation boundaries from target, claim, evidence, audience, action, observation, changed case, rejected failure, repair, consent, owner, and next-decision evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for public-identity threat and disclosure model after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose a public, selective, private, or live-only evidence channel from audience need, learner safety, and claim purpose.
Inventory personal, third-party, employer, client, research, secret, metadata, and inferred information before publication.
Model impersonation, harassment, discrimination, credential theft, doxxing, scraping, and relationship harm for the learner context.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Audience Purpose · changed case · Review Audience Purpose
steps.instruction:
review case — A public profile leaks private email, precise location, commit identity, client screenshots, API history, availability, and a protected personal detail. The review variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Choose a public, selective, private, or live-only evidence channel from audience need, learner safety, and claim purpose.

Which action demonstrates Audience Purpose without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Audience Purpose · changed case · Review Audience Purpose.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose a public, selective, private, or live-only evidence channel from audience need, learner safety, and claim purpose. For Audience Purpose · changed case · Review Audience Purpose, keep Audience Purpose connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A public profile leaks private email, precise location, commit identity, client screenshots, API history, availability, and a protected personal detail. The review variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Choose a public, selective, private, or live-only evidence channel from audience need, learner safety, and claim purpose.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Audience Purpose condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Web Content Accessibility Guidelines — Controls accessible portfolio and application artifacts while distinguishing automated checks from knowledgeable human and assistive-technology evaluation.

hints: For Audience Purpose · changed case · Review Audience Purpose, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Audience Purpose precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Audience Purpose · changed case · Review Audience Purpose, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A public profile leaks private email, precise location, commit identity, client screenshots, API history, availability, and a protected personal detail. The review variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Choose a public, selective, private, or live-only evidence channel from audience need, learner safety, and claim purpose.

For Audience Purpose · changed case · Review Audience Purpose, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: Serious candidates must maintain a maximally public personal brand.

options.text: Choose a public, selective, private, or live-only evidence channel from audience need, learner safety, and claim purpose. Apply it to the stated input and retain a reproducible trace for public-identity threat and disclosure model.
Serious candidates must maintain a maximally public personal brand. That claim fails the evidence boundary for Audience Purpose.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

stimulus.title: Publication Review case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A public profile leaks private email, precise location, commit identity, client screenshots, API history, availability, and a protected personal detail. The review variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Run content, history, secret, link, permission, accessibility, and changed-audience checks before and after publication.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A repository set private after exposure restores confidentiality.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Run content, history, secret, link, permission, accessibility, and changed-audience checks before and after publication. Artifact under review: public-identity threat and disclosure model.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Publication Review, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim fails the evidence boundary for Publication Review.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Record what required effort · Review Publication Review
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Publication Review: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A public profile leaks private email, precise location, commit identity, client screenshots, API history, availability, and a protected personal detail. The review variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Run content, history, secret, link, permission, accessibility, and changed-audience checks before and after publication.
